title: Unmarshalling error, can not remove container
body:
kind/bug

Briefly describe the problem you are having:

$ sudo podman rm nextcloud
Error: error unmarshalling container 1718cbba8ae35c6e838f3d84f61e5d22d85c95b562727308424b4bc811fee73d config: libpod.ContainerConfig.ContainerRootFSConfig: ImageVolumes: []*libpod.ContainerImageVolume: decode slice: expect [ or n, but found t, error found in #10 byte of ...|Volumes":true,"ShmDi|..., bigger context ...|.io/linuxserver/nextcloud:latest","imageVolumes":true,"ShmDir":"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-|...

That's what I get trying to perform many operations in podman (including ps, auto-update, etc). I'm using CentOS 8 up to date and the latest release of Podman from the project's repo, but I think this container and two others that produce similar errors were all made using the older version of Podman that is included with CentOS. I haven't tried going backwards to the older version because I am novice to intermediate in Linux sysadmin tasks and don't know how to reverse versions safely.

comments.body:
You are seeing #8613 - please revert to Podman 2.1.1 until Podman
2.2.1 can be released with a fix.

On Sun, Dec 6, 2020 at 7:07 PM WittyNameHere <notifications@github.com> wrote:
>
> Briefly describe the problem you are having:
>
> $ sudo podman rm nextcloud
> Error: error unmarshalling container 1718cbba8ae35c6e838f3d84f61e5d22d85c95b562727308424b4bc811fee73d config: libpod.ContainerConfig.ContainerRootFSConfig: ImageVolumes: []*libpod.ContainerImageVolume: decode slice: expect [ or n, but found t, error found in #10 byte of ...|Volumes":true,"ShmDi|..., bigger context ...|.io/linuxserver/nextcloud:latest","imageVolumes":true,"ShmDir":"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-|...
>
> That's what I get trying to perform many operations in podman (including ps, auto-update, etc). I'm using CentOS 8 up to date and the latest release of Podman from the project's repo, but I think this container and two others that produce similar errors were all made using the older version of Podman that is included with CentOS. I haven't tried going backwards to the older version because I am novice to intermediate in Linux sysadmin tasks and don't know how to reverse versions safely.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or unsubscribe.
Understood. Kubic's repo doesn't have 2.1.1, so if acceptable I'll just deal with seeing error messages during certain tasks. They don't refer to any containers I currently use.
`podman rm --force` should also be able to forcibly remove the containers even if they cannot be decoded properly
I agree, But I think there might be issues down in containers/storage.
